title: About the Living in Aotearoa survey | Stats NZ
content:
## About the Living in Aotearoa survey
Living in Aotearoa was a longitudinal survey designed to understand the everyday living costs of households throughout Aotearoa New Zealand.
In early 2024, the Government Statistician made the decision to discontinue the survey.

## What you'll find on this page:
- The decision to stop the Living in Aotearoa
- Surveying household living costs in 2024 and beyond
- Thank you to Living in Aotearoa participants
- Your information remains confidential
- Taking part in Stats NZ surveys
- Contact the Living in Aotearoa team

## The decision to stop the Living in Aotearoa survey
We are evolving the way we collect and produce data to ensure the ongoing sustainability of quality statistics.
Since its implementation, Living in Aotearoa has faced ongoing data collection challenges, made worse by the longitudinal nature of the survey, as well as environmental factors such as Covid-19 and adverse weather events, and increasing costs. In early 2024, the Government Statistician made the decision to stop the survey.

## Surveying household living costs in 2024 and beyond
We will produce information about household income, living costs, and persistent child poverty through data collected from an enhanced Household Economic Survey and using information already collected by other government agencies.
Participants selected for the Household Economic Survey will only need to be interviewed once, instead of once a year for six-years for the Living in Aotearoa survey.

## Thank you to Living in Aotearoa participants
We would like to thank the thousands of people, families and whānau that participated in Living in Aotearoa. The longitudinal survey required a significant commitment from participants, with our field staff interviewing every member of the household once a year for six years. We are truly grateful for that level of commitment.

## Your information remains confidential
The information participants provided as part of Living in Aotearoa, like any of our surveys, is kept private, secure, and confidential by Stats NZ.
Privacy, security, and confidentiality of survey data explains how we keep your data safe. While we won’t produce official statistics from the data Living in Aotearoa participants provided, the information is still being put to good use. It will be used to inform and develop our approach to collecting data and information about household living costs in the future.
About the Household Economic Survey has more information about why we carry out the survey.

## Taking part in Stats NZ surveys
If your household was taking part in the Living in Aotearoa survey you may still be selected for another Stats NZ survey in the future.
By taking part in Stats NZ our surveys, you provide decision-makers, organisations, business, and government with the information they need to improve the lives of people in your communities.

## Contact the Living in Aotearoa team
If you have any questions about the discontinuation of the Living in Aotearoa survey, please:
- email livinginaotearoa@stats.govt.nz
